element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players awaken in temple and discover it contains multiple worlds; serves as narrative and mechanical anchor point
Core movement and navigation mechanic established immediately
Standard JRPG locations introduced as player explores worlds within temple
Encounters yield experience points; progression gated by accumulated levels
Vertical progression system; unlocks spells and techniques at specific level thresholds
Learned automatically when experience reaches target levels; expands tactical options
First collectible ark; unlocks partner selection mechanic when obtained
Second key ark; enables player to recruit two partners from temple figurines

element_introduction_order.first_appears: After obtaining Power and Wisdom Arks
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players choose two allies from cast of six characters (Miriene, Reeshine, Lux, Kamiwoo, Meisia, Tokio); each tied to specific world storyline
Anthology structure; each world has unique narrative tied to character (witch, grappler, tatsujin, ogre, priestess, ninja)
Collection objective; finding all arks grants access to final temple area
End-game challenge; victory enables return home and game completion

typical_relief_triggers: Reaching towns for shopping and rest
Leveling up and learning new spells
typical_tension_triggers: Accumulating experience pressure to reach next level
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Episodic tension-release cycles tied to world completion; each of six worlds presents contained storyline arc with intro exploration, mid-game battles, and climactic ark collection, creating repetitive but varied rhythm across playthrough

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction in temple; basic navigation and early random battles establish mechanics gradually
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate escalation as player collects arks across six different worlds; each world introduces new storyline character and escalating enemy difficulty tied to experience gates
difficulty_curve.late_game: Dangerous phase after Power and Wisdom Ark acquisition; partner selection adds tactical depth; remaining arks become harder to obtain; final boss represents peak difficulty
notable_spikes: Transitioning between worlds (new enemy types and dungeon layouts)
Final boss encounter after collecting all arks

notable_level_patterns.description: Central temple serves as persistent hub; six distinct worlds branch outward, each containing towns, dungeons, and one collectible ark; players return to hub between world expeditions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Temple (hub) containing access points to all six worlds

notable_level_patterns.description: Each of six worlds anchored to specific character (Miriene witch, Reeshine grappler, Lux tatsujin, Kamiwoo ogre, Meisia priestess, Tokio ninja); creates narrative compartmentalization and pacing structure
Spell and technique acquisition locked behind experience thresholds; encourages random battle grinding to unlock new tactical options before dungeon challenges
Seven arks total (Power, Wisdom, plus five others); obtaining first two unlocks partner system; obtaining all seven opens final area; creates cascading unlock structure
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Final temple area (accessible only after collecting all arks)
